page: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual machine featuring a single shared vCPU based on the Intel Xeon Cascadelake architecture (x86_64) running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 2.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX-512, and AES cryptography instructions. No GPU is included in this configuration, and it comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance characteristics. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance scores are identical at 1365.08, reflecting the single-threaded nature of the shared vCPU. Memory bandwidth is measured at 96.65 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 8.8 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 7.23 GB/sec for large uncached writes. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 23.62 GB/sec, while bzip3 decompression performs at an average tier of 9.03 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are relatively low at 1669.57 and 943.72 respectively, and Redis SET operations achieve 1.56 million operations per second.

Given the shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, this server offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight, non-resource-intensive workloads. It is not designed for heavy computational tasks, large-scale databases, or parallel processing. Instead, the hardware profile is best suited for basic web serving, delivering 1.35 GB/sec of static web throughput, simple scripting, lightweight application hosting, development environments, or low-traffic utility services.
description: The Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-intel is an entry-level virtual machine from the High Performance family, featuring 1 shared vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show modest performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 1669.57 and a single-core stress-ng score of 1365.08. Memory bandwidth drops significantly for large uncached blocks, and database operations are limited. However, it maintains a steady static web serving throughput of 1.35 GB/sec and handles 1.56 million Redis SET operations per second. This server represents a highly budget-friendly option for lightweight utility tasks, basic web serving, and development environments where dedicated CPU resources are not required.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-intel is an entry-level virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, entry-level virtual server with shared Intel processing and local NVMe storage for lightweight utility workloads.

2.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
